title: Preserve Meaning with Reduced-Motion Alternatives

steps.title: 04 · Read the ethical launch status feedback and orbit illustration preference adaptation c · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves css reduced motion when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: ethical launch status feedback and orbit illustration preference adaptation.
steps.why: Preserve Meaning with Reduced-Motion Alternatives makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in ethical launch status feedback and orbit illustration preference adaptation.

content.text: Prefers-reduced-motion communicates a preference to reduce nonessential motion; it does not mean remove meaning or shorten every animation to nearly zero. Stop large spatial movement, parallax, zoom, rotation, and unnecessary loops. Preserve state through immediate text, icon, border, or opacity changes. The base should work when the query is unsupported, and motion controls remain useful regardless of system preference. Apply it to ethical launch status feedback and orbit illustration preference adaptation.
